Air purifier
triples.object: An air purifier or air cleaner is a device which removes contaminants from the air in a room to improve indoor air quality . These devices are commonly marketed to people with allergies or asthma , and at reducing or eliminating second-hand tobacco smoke .

triples.subject: HEPA filter
Allergies
Asthma
Indoor air quality
Particulate matter
Volatile organic compounds
triples.object: Volatile organic compounds ( VOCs ) are organic compounds that have a high vapor pressure at room temperature . [ 1 ] They are common and exist in a variety of settings and products, not limited to upholstered furniture , arts and crafts supplies, dry cleaned clothing, and cleaning supplies . [ 2 ]

triples.subject: Air pollution
Allergen
triples.object: An allergen is an otherwise harmless substance that triggers an allergic reaction in sensitive individuals by stimulating an immune response .

triples.subject: Air filtration
triples.object: A particulate air filter is a device composed of fibrous or porous materials which removes particulates such as smoke , dust , pollen , mold , viruses , and bacteria from the air . Filters containing an adsorbent or catalyst such as charcoal (carbon) may also remove odors and gaseous pollutants, suc

triples.subject: Airflow
triples.object: Airflow, or air flow , is the movement of air. Air behaves in a fluid manner, meaning particles naturally flow from areas of higher pressure to those where the pressure is lower. Atmospheric air pressure is directly related to altitude , temperature , and composition. [ 1 ]

triples.subject: Room air distribution
triples.object: Room air distribution is characterizing how air is introduced to, flows through, and is removed from spaces. [ 1 ] HVAC airflow in spaces generally can be classified by two different types: mixing (or dilution) and displacement .

triples.subject: Air cleaner
triples.object: A particulate air filter is a device composed of fibrous or porous materials which removes particulates such as smoke , dust , pollen , mold , viruses , and bacteria from the air . Filters containing an adsorbent or catalyst such as charcoal (carbon) may also remove odors and gaseous pollutants, suc

triples.subject: Air quality index
triples.object: An air quality index ( AQI ) is an approximation of how polluted the air currently is or how polluted it is forecast to become. As air pollution levels rise, so does the AQI, along with the associated public health risks.

triples.subject: Ultrafine particles
triples.object: Ultrafine particles ( UFPs ) are particulate matter of nanoscale size (less than 0.1 Î¼m or 100 nm in diameter). [ 1 ] Regulations do not exist for this size class of ambient air pollution particles, which are far smaller than the regulated PM 10 and PM 2.5 particle classes and are believed to have

triples.subject: Formaldehyde
Carbon monoxide
triples.object: Carbon monoxide ( chemical formula CO ) is a poisonous, flammable gas that is colorless, odorless, tasteless, and slightly less dense than air. Carbon monoxide consists of one carbon atom and one oxygen atom connected by a triple bond . It is the simplest carbon oxide . In coordination complexes , t

triples.subject: Nitrogen dioxide
triples.object: Dinitrogen tetroxide Dinitrogen trioxide Nitric oxide Nitrous oxide

triples.subject: Sulfur dioxide

Air pollution control
triples.object: Emission standards are the legal requirements governing air pollutants released into the atmosphere . Emission standards set quantitative limits on the permissible amount of specific air pollutants that may be released from specific sources over specific timeframes. They are generally designed to ac

triples.subject: Environmental health
triples.object: Environmental health is the branch of public health concerned with all aspects of the natural and built environment affecting human health. Its study determines the requirements for a healthy environment, with the goal of effective control over the factors that affect health. [ 1 ] The major sub-dis

triples.subject: Molekule
triples.object: Molekule is a science and technology company headquartered in San Francisco . It designs and manufactures air purifiers that use photoelectrochemical oxidation (PECO), a technology that the company claims may be useful against chemicals, microbes, allergens, and other forms of air pollution. [ 2 ] T

triples.subject: Smog tower
triples.object: Smog towers or smog free towers are structures designed as large-scale air purifiers to reduce air pollution particles ( smog ).  This approach to the problem of urban air pollution involves air filtration and removal of suspended mechanical particulates such as soot and requires energy or power.  A

triples.subject: ActivePure
triples.object: ActivePure Technologies, LLC is a private technology manufacturing company headquartered in Dallas , Texas. With its main facilities in Bristol, Virginia , the company creates air purifiers for distributors. Many of these products contain photohydrolysis-based purification cells, which the company r

[ 2 ] [ 3 ] [ 4 ] Founded and headquartered in Seoul , Coway is the largest water purifier company

triples.subject: Air sanitizer
triples.object: An air sanitizer is a sanitizer that acts on airborne microorganisms , including bacteria , fungi , and viruses , in households, institutions, and/or commercial environments.

triples.subject: Sharper Image
triples.object: Sharper Image is an American brand that offers consumers home electronics , air purifiers , gifts , and other lifestyle products through its website, catalog, and third-party retailers. The brand is owned by ThreeSixty Group, [ 1 ] with the U.S. catalog and website owned and operated by Michigan-bas

triples.subject: GPS Air
triples.object: GPS Air , formerly Global Plasma Solutions , [ 1 ] is an American indoor air quality company that provides air quality technologies for residential, commercial and industrial buildings with a focus on using "needlepoint bipolar ionization ", also known as soft ionization. The company produces in-duc

triples.subject: Respirator assigned protection factors
triples.object: The respiratory protective devices (RPD) can protect workers only if their protective properties are adequate to the conditions in the workplace . Therefore, specialists have developed criteria for the selection of proper, adequate respirators, including the Assigned Protection Factors (APF) - the d

triples.subject: Workplace respirator testing
triples.object: Respirators , also known as respiratory protective equipment (RPE) or respiratory protective devices (RPD), are used in some workplaces to protect workers from air contaminants. Initially, respirator effectiveness was tested in laboratories, but in the late 1960s it was found that these tests gave m

triples.subject: Middlings purifier
triples.object:
A middlings purifier is a device used in the production of flour to remove the husks from the kernels of wheat .  
It was developed in Minnesota by Edmund LaCroix, a French inventor hired by Cadwallader C. Washburn and George Christian of the Washburn "A" Mill . [ 1 ] It was developed to complement

triples.subject: Elastomeric respirator
triples.object:
Elastomeric respirators , also called reusable air-purifying respirators , [ 1 ] seal to the face with elastomeric material, which may be a natural or synthetic rubber . They are generally reusable.
Full-face versions of elastomeric respirators seal better and protect the eyes. [ 2 ]

triples.subject: Respiratory protective equipment
triples.object: Respiratory protective equipment ( RPE ), also called protective breathing equipment ( PBE ) in the US, [ 1 ] is a form of personal protective equipment designed to protect the wearer from a variety of airborne hazards in the form of a gas, fume, mist, dust or vapour. Respirators filter the air to r

triples.subject: Lava filter
triples.object: A lava filter is a biological filter that uses lavastone pebbles as support material on which microorganisms can grow in a thin biofilm . [ 1 ] This community of microorganisms, known as the periphyton , break down the odor components in the air, such as hydrogen sulfide . The biodegradation process

triples.subject: Hypoallergenic vacuum cleaner
triples.object:
A hypoallergenic vacuum cleaner is a vacuum cleaner with filters designed to retain the vast majority (ideally at least 99.97%) of particles within the vacuum system.
If successful this retention would greatly reduce the amount of potential allergens in the environment in which it is used.
Regular v

triples.subject: Ultra-low particulate air
triples.object: Ultra-low particulate air ( ULPA ) is a type of air filter . A ULPA filter can remove from the air at least 99.999% of dust , pollen , mold , bacteria and any airborne particles with a minimum particle penetration size of 120 nanometres (0.12 Î¼m , ultrafine particles ). A ULPA filter can removeât

triples.subject: Air filter
triples.object: A particulate air filter is a device composed of fibrous or porous materials which removes particulates such as smoke , dust , pollen , mold , viruses , and bacteria from the air . Filters containing an adsorbent or catalyst such as charcoal (carbon) may also remove odors and gaseous pollutants, suc

triples.subject: Air filtration in operating rooms
triples.object: Air filtration guidelines for operating rooms are determined by the American Society of Heating, Refrigerating and Air-Conditioning Engineers (ASHRAE) using a standard known as Minimum Efficiency Reporting Value (MERV). MERV is determined based on the size of particles successfully removed from the

triples.subject: Biosafety cabinet
triples.object: A biosafety cabinet ( BSC )âalso called a biological safety cabinet or microbiological safety cabinet âis an enclosed, ventilated laboratory workspace for safely working with materials contaminated with (or potentially contaminated with) pathogens requiring a defined biosafety level . Several di

triples.subject: Fan filter unit
triples.object: A fan filter unit ( FFU ) is a type of motorized air filtering equipment. It is used to supply purified air to cleanrooms , laboratories , medical facilities or microenvironments by removing airborne particles from recirculating air. [ 1 ] The units are installed within the system's ceiling or floor

triples.subject: Aerobiological engineering
triples.object: Aerobiological engineering is the science of designing buildings and systems to control airborne pathogens and allergens in indoor environments. The most-common environments include commercial buildings, residences and hospitals. This field of study is important because controlled indoor climates ge

triples.subject: Minimum efficiency reporting value
triples.object: Minimum Efficiency Reporting Value , commonly known as MERV , is a measurement scale designed in 1987 by the American Society of Heating, Refrigerating and Air-Conditioning Engineers ( ASHRAE ) to report the effectiveness of air filters in more detail than other ratings. For example, often a high-ef

triples.subject: Clean air delivery rate
triples.object: The clean air delivery rate ( CADR ) is a figure of merit that is the cubic feet per minute (CFM) of air that has had all the particles of a given size distribution removed. For air filters that have air flowing through them, it is the fraction of particles (of a particular size distribution) that h

triples.subject: Institute of Environmental Sciences and Technology
triples.object: The Institute of Environmental Sciences and Technology ( IEST ) is a non-profit, technical society where professionals who impact controlled environments connect, gain knowledge, receive advice, and work together to create industry best practices. The organization uniquely serves environmental test

triples.subject: Individually ventilated cage
triples.object: An individually ventilated cage (IVC) is used to keep an animal separated from other animals and possible exposures, including exposure by air.

triples.subject: Cleanroom
triples.object: A cleanroom or clean room is an engineered space that maintains a very low concentration of airborne particulates . It is well-isolated, well-controlled from contamination , and actively cleansed. Such rooms are commonly needed for scientific research and in industrial production for all nanoscale p
